title: poa annua subsp. exigua
intro: Poa annua subsp. exigua, also known as the annual bluegrass, is a subspecies of the Poa annua species, a type of grass that is widely distributed and recognized for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Poa annua subsp. exigua include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
